title: Resource Selection Algorithm
description: Algorithm to help students select appropriate Green IT learning resources based on their learning goals and current knowledge level
code:
ALGORITHM SelectGreenITResources
INPUT: learnerLevel, topicArea, availableTime
OUTPUT: recommendedResources

BEGIN
 IF learnerLevel = "beginner" THEN
 ADD "Textbooks: Green Computing Basics" TO resources
 ADD "Introductory Online Courses" TO resources
 ADD "Foundation Standards (ISO 14001 overview)" TO resources
 
 ELSE IF learnerLevel = "intermediate" THEN
 ADD "Advanced Textbooks" TO resources
 ADD "Case Studies Collection" TO resources
 ADD "Industry Reports and White Papers" TO resources
 ADD "Online Certifications" TO resources
 ADD "Technical Standards (ISO 50001)" TO resources
 
 ELSE IF learnerLevel = "advanced" THEN
 ADD "Peer-reviewed Journals" TO resources
 ADD "Conference Proceedings" TO resources
 ADD "Research Papers" TO resources
 ADD "Simulation Labs" TO resources
 ADD "Professional Workshops" TO resources
 
 ENDIF

 IF topicArea = "energy-efficiency" THEN
 ADD "Data Center Energy Guides" TO resources
 ADD "ISO 50001 Resources" TO resources
 
 ELSE IF topicArea = "e-waste" THEN
 ADD "E-waste Regulations Documents" TO resources
 ADD "Recycling Case Studies" TO resources
 
 ELSE IF topicArea = "sustainable-software" THEN
 ADD "Green Software Metrics Papers" TO resources
 ADD "Energy-aware Algorithm Research" TO resources
 
 ENDIF

 IF availableTime < 2 hours THEN
 ADD "Short Podcasts and Webinars" TO resources
 ELSE IF availableTime > 20 hours THEN
 ADD "Full Online Courses with Projects" TO resources
 ENDIF

 RETURN resources

END
explanation: This algorithm helps learners select appropriate resources based on their current level (beginner/intermediate/advanced), specific topic interest, and available study time. It demonstrates how a systematic approach can optimize learning resource selection for Green IT.

complexity.space: O(n) - stores list of recommended resources

title: Learning Progress Tracker
description: System to track progress across different types of learning resources in Green IT
code:
ALGORITHM TrackLearningProgress
INPUT: completedResources[], targetCompetencies[]
OUTPUT: progressReport, gaps[]

BEGIN
 SET progress = 0
 SET totalCategories = 4
 SET categories = ["Foundational", "Technical", "Practical", "Current"]
 SET categoryProgress = {Foundational: 0, Technical: 0, Practical: 0, Current: 0}

 FOR EACH resource IN completedResources DO
 IF resource.type = "textbook" OR "online-course" THEN
 categoryProgress[Foundational] += 25
 ENDIF
 
 IF resource.type = "standard" OR "technical-paper" THEN
 categoryProgress[Technical] += 25
 ENDIF
 
 IF resource.type = "case-study" OR "simulation" THEN
 categoryProgress[Practical] += 25
 ENDIF
 
 IF resource.type = "journal" OR "webinar" OR "conference" THEN
 categoryProgress[Current] += 25
 ENDIF
 ENDFOR

 FOR EACH category IN categories DO
 progress += categoryProgress[category]
 ENDFOR

 progress = progress / totalCategories

 FOR EACH competency IN targetCompetencies DO
 IF competency NOT IN completedResources THEN
 ADD competency TO gaps
 ENDIF
 ENDFOR

 RETURN progressReport = progress%, gaps[]

END
explanation: This algorithm helps students track their learning progress across four main categories: Foundational (textbooks/courses), Technical (standards/papers), Practical (case studies/labs), and Current (journals/conferences). It identifies gaps in learning to ensure comprehensive coverage.

title: Resource Quality Assessment
description: Algorithm to evaluate quality and credibility of Green IT learning resources
code:
ALGORITHM AssessResourceQuality
INPUT: resource
OUTPUT: qualityScore, recommendation

BEGIN
 SET score = 0
 SET maxScore = 100

 // Currency Check (25 points)
 IF resource.publicationYear >= currentYear - 2 THEN
 score += 25
 ELSE IF resource.publicationYear >= currentYear - 5 THEN
 score += 15
 ELSE
 score += 5
 ENDIF

 // Authority Check (25 points)
 IF resource.author = "recognized-institution" THEN
 score += 25
 ELSE IF resource.author = "verified-expert" THEN
 score += 20
 ELSE IF resource.author = "peer-reviewed" THEN
 score += 25
 ELSE
 score += 5
 ENDIF

 // Source Type Check (25 points)
 IF resource.sourceType = "journal" THEN
 score += 25
 ELSE IF resource.sourceType = "conference" THEN
 score += 20
 ELSE IF resource.sourceType = "textbook" THEN
 score += 15
 ELSE IF resource.sourceType = "website" THEN
 score += 10
 ENDIF

 // Relevance Check (25 points)
 IF resource.relevanceScore >= 8 THEN
 score += 25
 ELSE IF resource.relevanceScore >= 5 THEN
 score += 15
 ELSE
 score += 5
 ENDIF

 // Recommendation
 IF score >= 80 THEN
 recommendation = "Highly Recommended"
 ELSE IF score >= 60 THEN
 recommendation = "Recommended"
 ELSE IF score >= 40 THEN
 recommendation = "Use with Caution"
 ELSE
 recommendation = "Not Recommended"
 ENDIF

 RETURN qualityScore = score, recommendation

END
explanation: This algorithm helps evaluate Green IT resources based on four criteria: Currency (how recent), Authority (author credibility), Source Type (academic vs general), and Relevance (alignment with learning goals). It assigns a quality score and provides recommendation for resource selection.
